error_signature: Postgres node returns empty item for existing ID
title: Postgres node returns empty data for existing Telegram ID
symptom: The Postgres node executes successfully but returns an empty item for a known user ID, causing the workflow to loop indefinitely.
applies_when: Using Postgres node to fetch user data
root_cause: There is a mismatch between how Telegram IDs are handled (as large numbers) and the Postgres `Int8` column type. This inconsistency leads to failed comparisons in the SQL query, resulting in no data being returned.
fix_steps: Replace the 'Get User' node with an 'Initialize User' node using an SQL INSERT command.

Use the SQL command: INSERT INTO 'HP_Users' (telegram_id) VALUES ('{{ ($json.callback_query ? $json.callback_query.from.id : $json.message.chat.id).toString() }}') ON CONFLICT (telegram_id) DO UPDATE SET last_question_ts = NOW() RETURNING *;
agent_instruction: Implement the Atomic Upsert pattern to handle both user creation and retrieval in a single SQL command.
